ksa_text: Knowledge of operating system fundamentals and kernel concepts relevant to data engineering, including virtualization mechanisms, memory management and paging, process scheduling, I/O systems, file system operations, and resource allocation.

pass_2_evaluation.rationale: Technical Foundations falls outside the primary competency scope of the Data and AI Transformation Lead. This knowledge KSA does not align with the role's core responsibilities and is excluded.

pass_3_adversarial_internal.challenge: Seniority fit was marked 'too_junior', but the dimension is Knowledge — awareness-level KSAs are typically appropriate across seniority tiers.
pass_3_adversarial_internal.rebuttal: Even Knowledge-level KSAs that describe specific low-level technical details (e.g., implementation internals) are not expected at the senior specialist level. Exclusion upheld.
pass_3_adversarial_internal.ruling: Exclusion upheld: Knowledge KSAs with predominantly execution-oriented framing are appropriately excluded at the senior specialist tier.

pass_4_adversarial_external.challenge: Is Technical Foundations truly out-of-scope for the Data and AI Transformation Lead? Emerging role definitions may include broader competency expectations.
pass_4_adversarial_external.evidence: Current job market analysis and framework definitions (DCWF, DDAT, NICE) do not list Technical Foundations as a core or recommended competency for the Data and AI Transformation Lead. Role scope exclusion is consistent with external evidence.
pass_4_adversarial_external.ruling: Upheld: external evidence confirms Technical Foundations falls outside the established competency profile for the Data and AI Transformation Lead. Exclusion maintained.

decision_rationale: Technical Foundations is outside the competency scope of the Data and AI Transformation Lead. All evaluation passes consistently support exclusion.
